verses.commentary: To disciples: 'Temptations to sin (skandala, σκάνδαλα) are sure to come — but woe to the one through whom they come.' Inevitability does not cancel responsibility.
Better for him a millstone hung around the neck and cast into the sea than to cause one of these little ones to sin. Severity of stumbling-block-creation toward the small.
'Pay attention to yourselves. If your brother sins, rebuke him; if he repents, forgive him.' Confrontation and reception together.
Seven times in a day sins against you, seven times turns to you saying 'I repent' — forgive him. Repeated forgiveness for repeated repentance.
The apostles to the Lord: 'Increase our faith.' Honest plea — the standard feels impossible.

'If you had faith like a grain of mustard seed, you could say to this mulberry tree, Be uprooted and planted in the sea, and it would obey.' Mustard-seed faith is sufficient. Quantity is not the question.
Which of you with a servant plowing or keeping sheep — when he returns from the field, will say, Come at once and recline at table?
Will he not rather say, Prepare supper for me — dress for service — serve me — afterwards you can eat and drink?
Does he thank the servant because he did what was commanded? Hardly.
'So you also, when you have done all that you were commanded, say, We are unworthy servants — we have only done what was our duty.' No meritorious surplus. Obedience is the floor, not the ceiling.
On the way to Jerusalem, passing along between Samaria and Galilee. Border region.
Entering a village — ten lepers met him, who stood at a distance (Leviticus 13:46 requirement).
Lifted their voices: 'Jesus, Master, have mercy on us.' Collective petition.
Seeing them, he said: 'Go and show yourselves to the priests.' Faith-act before evidence. As they went — they were cleansed.
One of them, seeing he was healed, turned back, glorifying God with a loud voice.
Fell on his face at Jesus' feet, giving him thanks. And he was a Samaritan (Samarites, Σαμαρίτης). The Lukan twist — the outsider returns; the insiders go on.
'Were not ten cleansed? Where are the nine?'

'Was no one found to return and give praise to God except this foreigner (allogenes, ἀλλογενής)?' The label normally used to keep Gentiles out of the temple — here applied to one whose gratitude exceeds insiders.
'Rise and go — your faith has saved you (sesoken se, σέσωκέν σε).' The Samaritan receives the salvation-word; the nine received only healing. Faith expressed in gratitude reaches deeper.

Pharisees asked when the kingdom of God would come: 'The kingdom of God is not coming with things that can be observed (meta parateresos, μετὰ παρατηρήσεως — close watching).' Not a spectacle to be timed.
Nor will they say 'Look, here it is' or 'There!' — for the kingdom of God is among you (entos hymon, ἐντὸς ὑμῶν — within or among). Present-tense kingdom, possibly in Jesus' person.
To disciples: 'The days are coming when you will desire to see one of the days of the Son of Man — and you will not see it.' Future longing predicted.
'They will say, Look, there! Look, here! Do not go out or follow them.' Same warning as Matthew 24:23.
As lightning that flashes lights up the sky from one side to the other — so will the Son of Man be in his day. Universal visibility.
'But first he must suffer many things and be rejected by this generation.' Cross precedes glory.
As it was in the days of Noah, so it will be in the days of the Son of Man. Paradigm warning.
Eating, drinking, marrying — until the day Noah entered the ark, the flood came and destroyed them all. Normal-life-amid-warning.
Same in the days of Lot — eating, drinking, buying, selling, planting, building.
On the day Lot went out of Sodom — fire and sulfur rained from heaven, destroyed them all. Two paradigms reinforce the warning.
'So will it be on the day the Son of Man is revealed.' Sudden, against a backdrop of normalcy.
On that day — whoever is on the housetop with goods in the house, let him not come down to take them; one in the field — likewise, let him not turn back. No detour for possessions.
'Remember Lot's wife.' Two-word warning. Looking back to what was lost cost her everything.
Whoever seeks to preserve his life will lose it; whoever loses it will keep it alive (zogonesei, ζῳογονήσει — preserve alive). Paradox restated.
That night — two in one bed; one taken, one left. Sudden separation.
Two women grinding together — one taken, one left. Same pattern, different setting.
[Textual variant absent from earliest manuscripts. Later manuscripts add: 'Two men will be in the field; one will be taken and the other left.' Harmonization with Matthew 24:40.]
'Where, Lord?' 'Where the corpse is, there the vultures (or eagles, aetoi, ἀετοί) will be gathered.' Cryptic — judgment naturally locates itself where it is required.
